req.title: Hr-Admin creates expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to create a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven creation of an expense-claim by an hr-admin. I identified four test axes: (1) EP positive – all mandatory fields supplied by an authenticated hr-admin yields 200/201 and an audit record; (2) NEG – submitting the form with a mandatory field omitted yields a 400 validation error and no entity is persisted; (3) BVA – amount field at its minimum accepted boundary (e.g. 0.01) passes validation; (4) NEG/STC – an unauthenticated request is rejected with 401; (5) DT – a user with an insufficient role (employee, not hr-admin) is rejected with 403. This covers all stated error pathways and the happy path.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_creates_expense_claim_all_fields_valid
preconditions: A valid expense-claim payload with all mandatory fields is prepared
actions: POST /expense-claims with all mandatory fields and valid hr-admin credentials
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the created expense-claim id
trace: R-WEB-041: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_creates_expense_claim_all_fields_valid(
    hr_admin_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for hr-admin
    audit_log_query,       # <helper_fn> queries audit log by entity id
    cleanup_expense_claim  # <fixture> deletes created claim after test
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Team lunch",
        "amount": 45.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "meals"
    }

    response = hr_admin_client.post("/expense-claims", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("expense_claim_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"

    cleanup_expense_claim(entity_id)

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "At least one audit record must exist for the new claim"
    assert audit_entries[0]["action"] == "CREATE"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_rejected_when_mandatory_field_missing
preconditions: Payload intentionally omits the mandatory 'amount' field
expected: Response body contains a field-level validation error referencing 'amount'
trace: R-WEB-041: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expense_claim_rejected_when_mandatory_field_missing(
    hr_admin_client,     # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for hr-admin
    expense_claim_exists # <helper_fn> checks persistence by title lookup
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Missing amount claim",
        # 'amount' intentionally omitted
        "currency": "USD",
        "date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "meals"
    }

    response = hr_admin_client.post("/expense-claims", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 for missing mandatory field, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    error_body = response.json()
    errors_text = str(error_body).lower()
    assert "amount" in errors_text, (
        "Validation error message must reference the missing 'amount' field"
    )

    persisted = expense_claim_exists(title="Missing amount claim")
    assert not persisted, "No expense-claim must be stored when validation fails"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_amount_at_minimum_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Minimum accepted amount is 0.01 (smallest positive monetary unit)
actions: POST /expense-claims with amount set to 0.01 (minimum boundary)
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
trace: R-WEB-041: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-041: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expense_claim_amount_at_minimum_boundary_accepted(
    hr_admin_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for hr-admin
    cleanup_expense_claim  # <fixture> deletes created claim after test
):
    MIN_AMOUNT = 0.01

    payload = {
        "title": "Boundary min amount claim",
        "amount": MIN_AMOUNT,
        "currency": "USD",
        "date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "other"
    }

    response = hr_admin_client.post("/expense-claims", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Minimum boundary amount {MIN_AMOUNT} must be accepted; "
        f"got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("expense_claim_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"

    persisted_amount = body.get("amount")
    assert float(persisted_amount) == MIN_AMOUNT, (
        f"Persisted amount must equal {MIN_AMOUNT}, got {persisted_amount}"
    )

    cleanup_expense_claim(entity_id)

tests.name: test_expense_claim_rejected_when_unauthenticated
actions: POST /expense-claims with a valid payload but without any auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expense_claim_rejected_when_unauthenticated(
    anonymous_client,    # <fixture> HTTP client with no auth credentials
    expense_claim_exists # <helper_fn> checks persistence by title lookup
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Unauth claim attempt",
        "amount": 10.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "travel"
    }

    response = anonymous_client.post("/expense-claims", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated request, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    persisted = expense_claim_exists(title="Unauth claim attempt")
    assert not persisted, "No expense-claim must be stored for unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_rejected_for_insufficient_role
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A valid expense-claim payload is prepared
actions: POST /expense-claims as an authenticated employee (non-admin) user
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expense_claim_rejected_for_insufficient_role(
    employee_client,     # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for role=employee
    expense_claim_exists # <helper_fn> checks persistence by title lookup
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Forbidden role claim",
        "amount": 20.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "meals"
    }

    response = employee_client.post("/expense-claims", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for forbidden role, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    persisted = expense_claim_exists(title="Forbidden role claim")
    assert not persisted, "No expense-claim must be stored when role is insufficient"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: An expense-claim in DRAFT state already exists for the hr-admin
actions: POST /expense-claims with status='submitted' to transition from DRAFT to SUBMITTED
Capture response status and persisted state
expected: Audit record reflects the DRAFT -> SUBMITTED transition
trace: R-WEB-041: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_expense_claim_state_transition_draft_to_submitted(
    hr_admin_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for hr-admin
    audit_log_query,       # <helper_fn> queries audit log by entity id
    cleanup_expense_claim  # <fixture> deletes created claim after test
):
    payload = {
        "title": "State transition claim",
        "amount": 75.50,
        "currency": "USD",
        "date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "travel",
        "status": "submitted"
    }

    response = hr_admin_client.post("/expense-claims", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("expense_claim_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"

    assert body.get("status") == "submitted", (
        f"Expected status 'submitted', got {body.get('status')}"
    )

    cleanup_expense_claim(entity_id)

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(entity_id)
    assert any(e.get("action") == "CREATE" for e in audit_entries), (
        "Audit log must record a CREATE action for the submitted claim"
    )
